title: When AI Search Keeps the Click

turns.speaker: host
turns.text: This is The Daily Index, a concise conversation about the technology shaping the web. Today: Google AI Search, and what changes when answers arrive before a user visits the source.

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.text: Google's conversational interface is keeping people inside Search for longer, while independent websites receive less traffic from traditional results. That changes the exchange that helped the open web grow.

turns.speaker: host
turns.text: What is the technical mechanism behind that shift?

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.text: Instead of presenting a list of links, the system synthesizes information into a direct response. The user can ask follow-up questions inside the same conversational interface, so fewer sessions continue to the independent websites that supplied useful material.

turns.speaker: host
turns.text: So this is not only an S E O issue?

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.text: Right. It is a distribution issue. That matters. When discovery, explanation, and follow-up all happen in one Google AI Search experience, publishers can remain visible as sources while receiving less of the audience movement that supports their work.

turns.speaker: host
turns.text: And the natural transition is from measuring rankings to measuring whether attention ever leaves the platform.

turns.speaker: analyst
turns.text: Exactly. The central question is whether AI-assisted discovery can remain useful while preserving a meaningful path to the independent web. That is today's Daily Index.
